title: Priority Queue

statement.markdown:
## Priority Queue

A priority queue is a queue data structure that dequeues elements in priority order rather than insertion order. Implement a priority queue in TypeScript backed by a binary heap.

constraints: Your code must be written in TypeScript
Your code must implement the enqueue, dequeue, and peek functions
Your code must handle duplicate elements correctly
Your code must handle edge cases such as empty queue
Your code must pass all the test cases provided

gradingCriteria.description: The priority queue is implemented correctly, following the rules of a priority queue
The enqueue function adds elements correctly to the priority queue
The dequeue function removes elements correctly from the priority queue
The peek function returns the item with the highest priority without removing it
The code handles edge cases such as an empty queue correctly
